title: Dose API V2 2023-10 support query the value of a formula column?
excerpt:
I’ve notice the update of the API V2. 
  
I’m really want to know is that possible to query the value of the formula column?

posts.author: OluMel (Leo)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve notice the update of the API V2.

image1481×1382 158 KB

I’m really want to know is that possible to query the value of the formula column?

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there an update on this? Can I use this via make?

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Soroh This documentation is somewhat erroneous. While it does return a value, it is always null.

At this time the API does not return a value for formula columns, so it cannot be used via make. Lets hope next year!

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there any solution to this?

I need to run this formula through 15,000 subitems every night + when status changes. Doing it via make is very costly. I’m currently doing it via Monday formula but I need to copy it over to a status column so I can use the results to trigger automations, charts, etc.

IF({Category}=“HCBS Disenrolled”,“N/A”,

IF(OR(AND({Effective Duration#Labels}<>“Manual”, {Name}<>“CANS Assessment”, {Name}<>“CCF SED Verification Form”), AND({Effective Duration#Labels}=“Manual”,DAYS({Due Date}, {Item Date})>=60),AND({Effective Duration#Labels}=“Manual”, {Item Date}=“”)),

IF(DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))<-1, “Expired”, IF(AND(DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))>=-1, DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))<2), “2 Days”, IF(AND(DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))>=2, DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))<6), “1 Week”, IF(AND(DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))>=6, DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))<13), “2 Weeks”, IF(DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))>=13, “Completed”, IF({Due Date}=“”, “No Expiration”, “Error”)))))),

IF(AND({Effective Duration#Labels}=“Manual”, DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE({Item Date}))<60), “Update Expiration Date”,

IF(OR({Name}=“CCF SED Verification Form”, {Name}=“CANS Assessment”),

IF(DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))<-1, “Expired”, IF(AND(DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))>=-1, DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))<2), “2 Days”, IF(AND(DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))>=2, DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))<6), “1 Week”, IF(AND(DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))>=6, DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))<13), “2 Weeks”, IF(AND(DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))>=13, DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))<30),“1 Month”, IF(AND(DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))>=30, DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))<60),“2 Months”, IF(AND({Name}=“CANS Assessment”, DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))>=60, DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))<92),“3 Months”, IF(AND({Name}=“CANS Assessment”, DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))>=92), “Completed”, IF(AND({Name}=“CCF SED Verification Form”, DAYS({Due Date},FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))>=60), “Completed”, “Error”

)))))))))

)

)))

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: This is not possible without using a third party formula app that you create formulas in, and then that writes a value to a column. It doesn’t rely on the formula columns at all, since they have no value outside of your browser window.

posts.author: dvdsmpsn (David @ David Simpson Apps)
posts.cookedHtml: Currently this is targeted release in API v2025-01.

posts.author: hellotimi (David Kalu (Formula Pro - Use formula column results in automations!))
posts.cookedHtml:
The Formula PRO on the monday.com marketplace integrates seamlessly with the new Formula in Automations API, taking your native formula columns to the next level. This means you can now leverage powerful automations while working with the native formula functions without needing to learn new syntax. So you can perform the actions you described.

You can also project and write the results from your native formula columns to other columns (including those not supported by the native formula column), as well as to mirror columns and subitems. The integration with the API makes it easier than ever to automate and enhance your workflows.

Plus, Formula PRO includes 40 automation recipes, with new ones added every two weeks, allowing you to automate more tasks and streamline your processes.

Check it out here: Formula PRO: Advanced Automations for Native Formula Columns, Mirror Columns & Subitems!
